summary.rationale: This is a mid-to-large healthcare portal with HIPAA compliance requirements, complex role-based access, and integrations with two external EHR systems. The feature set is well-defined but several areas (telehealth, insurance verification) carry significant technical uncertainty. The hour range reflects the gap between a smooth integration path and the more likely scenario of EHR API quirks and compliance review cycles.
keyAssumptions: Client will provide EHR sandbox credentials within the first two weeks.
HIPAA compliance review is handled by a third-party auditor, not built in-house.
Telehealth video is powered by an existing SDK (e.g., Twilio, Daily.co), not custom WebRTC.
Design system and brand assets are provided upfront — no design exploration phase.
The mobile experience is responsive web, not a native app.

features.name: Patient Dashboard
features.description: Personalized landing page showing upcoming appointments, recent lab results, active prescriptions, and unread messages from care team.
assumptions: Dashboard widgets are pre-defined, not user-configurable.
Lab results are pulled from the EHR, not entered manually.

features.name: Appointment Scheduling
features.description: Book, reschedule, and cancel appointments with provider availability lookup and timezone handling.
assumptions: Provider availability comes from the EHR scheduling API.
No waitlist or auto-suggest functionality in v1.

features.name: Secure Messaging
features.description: HIPAA-compliant messaging between patients and care team members with attachment support and read receipts.
assumptions: Messages are stored encrypted at rest with AES-256.
Attachments are limited to images and PDFs under 10MB.
No real-time typing indicators — polling or push-based delivery only.

features.name: Telehealth Video Visits
features.description: Browser-based video consultations with waiting room, screen sharing, and session recording consent flow.
assumptions: Uses a third-party video SDK (Twilio or Daily.co).
Session recording is opt-in and stored externally.

features.name: Insurance Verification
features.description: Verify patient insurance eligibility in real-time via payer API integration, display coverage details, and flag discrepancies.
assumptions: Integration with a single eligibility clearinghouse (e.g., Availity).
Manual override available if API is unavailable.

features.name: Authentication & RBAC
features.description: Multi-factor authentication, role-based access control for patients, providers, and admin staff, session management with HIPAA-compliant timeouts.
assumptions: Uses an identity provider (Auth0 or Okta) — not built from scratch.

features.name: Lab Results Viewer
features.description: Display lab results from EHR with trend charts, reference ranges, and plain-language explanations powered by AI.
assumptions: Lab data follows HL7 FHIR format from the EHR.
AI explanations are generated at read-time, not pre-computed.

features.name: Prescription Management
features.description: View active and past prescriptions, request refills, and receive renewal reminders.
assumptions: Refill requests are sent to the provider — no e-prescribing in v1.
Prescription data pulled from EHR, read-only.

risks.title: EHR API instability and rate limits
risks.mitigation: Build a caching layer and circuit breaker pattern for EHR calls. Negotiate rate limit increases early. Budget 2 extra sprint days for API quirks.

risks.title: Telehealth scope creep into group calls and recording management
risks.mitigation: Lock v1 scope to 1:1 calls with opt-in recording. Defer group calls and recording management to v2.

risks.title: Delayed EHR sandbox credentials from client
risks.mitigation: Start with mock EHR data and build an adapter layer. Escalate credential request in week 1 kickoff.

risks.title: HIPAA compliance review extends timeline by 2-4 weeks
risks.mitigation: Engage the compliance auditor in parallel with development, not sequentially. Budget a 2-week buffer.

risks.title: Healthcare domain expertise gap on the team
risks.mitigation: Assign a healthcare-experienced tech lead or bring in a domain consultant for the first 3 sprints.

roles.reasoning: Owns architecture, EHR integration strategy, and HIPAA compliance coordination.
Core feature development across patient dashboard, scheduling, messaging, and prescriptions.
Telehealth UI, responsive design, and accessibility compliance.
HIPAA-focused test plans, integration test coverage for EHR flows, and security testing.
Healthcare UX patterns, accessibility standards, and patient-facing design that balances clarity with information density.

teamRecommendation.reasoning: A 6-person team balances velocity with the coordination overhead of healthcare compliance. The senior tech lead is critical for navigating EHR integrations and HIPAA requirements. Two mid-level full-stack engineers handle the bulk of feature work while a dedicated frontend engineer focuses on the video and responsive experiences.

comparableProjects.name: MedConnect Clinic Portal
comparableProjects.similarityRationale: Nearly identical scope — patient portal with EHR integration, messaging, and telehealth. Went 18% over estimate due to EHR API issues, which informed our risk assessment.

comparableProjects.name: CareLink Patient App
comparableProjects.similarityRationale: Larger scope (included native mobile) but same core feature set. Came in under estimate because the team had prior healthcare experience. Good reference for team composition.

comparableProjects.name: FinWell Benefits Portal
comparableProjects.similarityRationale: Different domain but similar compliance requirements (SOC 2), role-based access, and third-party API integrations. Useful baseline for the authentication and insurance verification features.

comparableProjects.name: TeleDoc Virtual Clinic
comparableProjects.similarityRationale: Focused telehealth-only build with scheduling integration. Smaller scope but strong signal for the video visit and appointment scheduling estimates specifically.

questions.question: Which EHR system(s) does the client use? Epic, Cerner, Allscripts, or another provider?
questions.affectedScope: EHR integration, Lab Results Viewer, Appointment Scheduling, Prescription Management

questions.question: Is HIPAA BAA (Business Associate Agreement) already in place with a cloud provider, or does that need to be established?
Does the client have an existing patient identity verification process, or should the portal include identity proofing?
questions.answer: Client confirmed they use LexisNexis for identity proofing — we integrate via API.

questions.question: Are there specific accessibility standards beyond WCAG 2.1 AA that apply (e.g., Section 508)?
questions.affectedScope: All frontend features, QA test plans

questions.question: Should session recordings from telehealth visits be stored within the portal or in the client's existing medical records system?
